paper.title: Spectroscopic Follow-Up of Candidate Runaway Stars

review.summary: This paper presents spectroscopic follow-up observations of candidate runaway stars. It contains nine internal tables: a sample summary with Gaia astrometry and spectral types; radial velocity observation logs for single-lined and double-lined objects; a summary of RV information with binary flags; orbital elements and derived properties for SB1 and SB2 systems; a table of "Magnificent Seven" neutron stars used for traceback calculations; and Gaia DR3 non-single star model parameters for comparison. Multiple tables are noted as available in machine-readable form. External resources include generic database acknowledgements (Gaia, SIMBAD, VizieR, WDS) that were removed, and a Gaia DR2 mission technical document URL retained.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents the basic astrometric, photometric, and kinematic properties of the target sample observed spectroscopically.

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Target name (HIP designation)

columns.name: Gaia ID
columns.meaning: Gaia DR3 source identifier

columns.name: \colhead\pi_\rm DR3 | (mas)
columns.meaning: Gaia DR3 parallax

columns.name: \Delta\pi | (mas)
columns.meaning: Additive zero-point parallax correction

columns.name: \colhead\mu_\alpha\cos\delta | \colhead(mas yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension (cos delta corrected)

columns.name: \colhead\mu_\delta | \colhead(mas yr^-1)
columns.meaning: Proper motion in declination

columns.name: RUWE
columns.meaning: Renormalised Unit Weight Error from Gaia DR3

columns.name: G | (mag)
columns.meaning: Gaia DR3 G-band magnitude

columns.name: SpT
columns.meaning: Spectral type from Tetzlaff+2011 or SIMBAD

columns.name: \colheadV_\rm LSR | \colhead(km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Space velocity relative to the Local Standard of Rest

internal_tables.comments: Table is noted as available in its entirety in machine-readable form.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarizes the radial-velocity information for the full sample, including binary classification flags used for selecting objects for orbit determination.

columns.meaning: Number of RV observations

columns.name: Error | (\kms)

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents orbital elements and derived physical properties for single-lined spectroscopic binaries in the sample.

source_refs.caption: Orbital Elements and Derived Properties for the Single-lined Binaries in the Sample. \label{tab:sb1}
source_refs.label: tab:sb1

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Target name

columns.name: P | (days)
columns.meaning: Orbital period

columns.name: \gamma | (\kms)
columns.meaning: Systemic (centre-of-mass) velocity

columns.name: K_1 | (\kms)
columns.meaning: Semi-amplitude of the primary radial velocity curve

columns.name: e
columns.meaning: Orbital eccentricity

columns.name: \omega_1 | (deg)
columns.meaning: Argument of periastron for the primary

columns.name: T_0 (BJD) | (2,400,000+)
columns.meaning: Reference time of periastron passage (eccentric) or maximum primary velocity (circular)

columns.name: f(M) | \colhead(M_\sun)
columns.meaning: Binary mass function

columns.name: M_2 \sin i | \colhead(M_\sun)
columns.meaning: Coefficient of the minimum secondary mass (M2 sin i)

columns.name: a_1 \sin i | (10^6 km)
columns.meaning: Projected semi-major axis of the primary orbit

columns.name: \sigma (\kms) | \colheadN_\rm obs
columns.meaning: RMS residual velocity and number of observations

columns.name: F
columns.meaning: Multiplicative scale factor applied to internal errors to achieve reduced chi^2 near unity

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Collects Gaia DR3 non-single star model orbital parameters for the targets, for comparison with the authors' own spectroscopic orbits.
